title: lepturus calcareus
intro: Lepturus calcareus, also known as the beach foxtail, is a type of grass that grows in coastal areas and is characterized by its distinctive foxtail-like spikes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Lepturus calcareus include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and relieving symptoms of fever. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions.
